questions.q: Universal adult franchise means the right to vote is granted to which group?
questions.explanation: Universal adult franchise grants the right to vote to every adult citizen.

questions.q: Under universal adult franchise in India, the right to vote does NOT depend on which of the following?
questions.explanation: The right to vote is granted regardless of caste, religion, gender, wealth or education.

questions.q: What is the current minimum voting age in India?
questions.explanation: The minimum voting age in India is 18 years.

questions.q: The voting age in India was lowered from 21 to 18 by which constitutional amendment?
questions.explanation: The 61st Amendment lowered the voting age from 21 to 18 years.

questions.q: In which year was the voting age lowered from 21 to 18 years?
questions.explanation: The voting age was lowered from 21 to 18 years in 1988.

questions.q: Universal adult franchise is sometimes summarised by which phrase?
questions.explanation: Universal adult franchise embodies the principle of 'one citizen, one vote'.

questions.q: Universal adult franchise was provided by India from which point?
options: The commencement of the Constitution in 1950
questions.explanation: The Constitution provided for universal adult franchise from its commencement in 1950.

questions.q: Under universal adult franchise, women in India received the right to vote on what basis relative to men?
questions.explanation: Women received the right to vote on equal terms with men from the start of the republic.

questions.q: Which body prepares and maintains the electoral rolls that give effect to universal adult franchise?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission of India prepares and maintains the electoral rolls.

questions.q: Universal adult franchise is a foundational feature of which type of system?
questions.explanation: Universal adult franchise is a foundational feature of a democratic system of government.

questions.q: Which article of the Constitution provides that elections to the Lok Sabha and state assemblies shall be on the basis of adult suffrage?
questions.explanation: Article 326 provides that elections to the Lok Sabha and Legislative Assemblies shall be on the basis of adult suffrage.

questions.q: Before the 61st Amendment, Article 326 originally set the minimum voting age at what?
questions.explanation: Article 326 originally fixed the minimum voting age at 21 years.

questions.q: India's adoption of universal adult franchise at independence was notable because many older democracies had introduced it how?
options: All at once in one year
questions.explanation: Unlike many older democracies that extended the vote gradually, India adopted universal adult franchise at the outset.

questions.q: The first general election conducted on the basis of universal adult franchise in India was held during which years?
questions.explanation: The first general election under universal adult franchise was held in 1951-52.

questions.q: Article 326 disqualifies a person from being registered as a voter on grounds such as non-residence, unsoundness of mind, crime, or which other ground?
questions.explanation: Article 326 allows disqualification on grounds of non-residence, unsoundness of mind, crime, or corrupt or illegal practice.

questions.q: Who was the first Chief Election Commissioner who organised the first general elections under universal adult franchise?
questions.explanation: Sukumar Sen, the first Chief Election Commissioner, organised the first general elections of 1951-52.

questions.q: The 61st Amendment, which lowered the voting age, is also formally known by which name?
questions.explanation: The amendment is formally the Constitution (Sixty-first Amendment) Act, 1988.

questions.q: Universal adult franchise ensures that the value of every adult citizen's vote is what?
questions.explanation: Under universal adult franchise, every adult citizen's vote carries equal value.

questions.q: Universal adult franchise applies to elections of the Lok Sabha and which other bodies through direct election?
questions.explanation: Direct elections by adult suffrage apply to the Lok Sabha and the State Legislative Assemblies under Article 326.

questions.q: The principle that a single electoral roll is maintained for each constituency, without separate rolls by community, supports which idea?
questions.explanation: India adopted a common electoral roll and a single undivided electorate, rejecting separate electorates.

questions.q: The right to vote in India is best described in legal terms as which of the following?
options: A fundamental right under Part III
questions.explanation: The right to vote is generally regarded as a constitutional and statutory right rather than a fundamental right under Part III.

questions.q: The 61st Amendment that lowered the voting age came into force in which year?
questions.explanation: Though passed in 1988, the 61st Amendment came into force in 1989, ahead of that year's general election.

questions.q: The detailed qualifications and procedures for voter registration under universal adult franchise are set out chiefly in which law?
options: The Representation of the People Act, 1950
questions.explanation: The Representation of the People Act, 1950 governs the preparation of electoral rolls and voter qualifications.

questions.q: In the Constituent Assembly, universal adult franchise was adopted despite concerns about which prevailing condition at the time?
questions.explanation: The Assembly adopted universal adult franchise despite widespread illiteracy among the population.

questions.q: Article 325 complements universal adult franchise by providing that no person shall be excluded from a general electoral roll on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex or which other factor?
questions.explanation: Article 325 bars exclusion from the general electoral roll on grounds of religion, race, caste or sex, and provides for one general roll for every constituency.

questions.q: The qualifying date with reference to which a person's age is determined for inclusion in the electoral roll is set by which authority?
questions.explanation: The qualifying date for determining voter age is fixed by law made by Parliament under the Representation of the People Act.

questions.q: The decision to grant universal adult franchise from the start is often linked to the egalitarian vision reflected in which part of the Constitution?
options: The Preamble's promise of political justice and equality
questions.explanation: Universal adult franchise reflects the Preamble's commitment to political justice and equality of status and opportunity.

questions.q: The first general election under universal adult franchise had an electorate of roughly how many eligible voters?
questions.explanation: The first general election of 1951-52 had over 170 million eligible voters.

questions.q: Universal adult franchise in India deliberately rejected qualifications based on literacy and property that had restricted the vote under which earlier framework?
questions.explanation: Earlier colonial-era arrangements had limited the franchise by property and education, which universal adult franchise abolished.

questions.q: Lowering the voting age to 18 was intended primarily to enfranchise which section of the population?
questions.explanation: The 61st Amendment lowered the voting age to 18 in order to enfranchise the country's youth.
